meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't monetize skills (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't monetize skills. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Starter Kit to Monetize Faster
content.seo_description: Stop struggling to monetize your coding skills. Use this intermediate checklist to build high-converting SaaS apps faster using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: The disconnect between your coding talent and your revenue lies in your deployment speed. By leveraging a high-performance SaaS starter kit, you shift from manual boilerplate to feature-complete delivery. Use Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative refinement to ship client-ready applications in days, not months.
content.intro:
You are staring at a graveyard of half-finished side projects while your clients demand production-ready SaaS apps yesterday. The 'can't monetize' trap isn't a failure of skill; it is a failure of velocity. You are spending your billable hours reinventing authentication and database schemas instead of building the features that actually drive MRR.

It is time to stop building from zero. By adopting a hardened SaaS starter kit, you treat your architecture as a product rather than a chore. This is the transition from 'coder' to 'AI Builder'—where you use machine-assisted workflows to outpace the market and finally turn your technical proficiency into a scalable income stream.

steps.title: Architect the Core Infrastructure
steps.description: Define the foundational stack (Next.js, Supabase, Stripe) to ensure your starter kit is production-ready from the first commit.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a high-performance Next.js 14 boilerplate with App Router, Tailwind CSS, and Shadcn UI. Include pre-configured Supabase auth and Stripe subscription integration.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Initialize project structure with Next.js and Supabase auth'

steps.title: Automate Database Schema Design
steps.description: Use AI to normalize your data models, ensuring your SaaS can handle multi-tenant scaling without manual migration headaches.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a multi-tenant PostgreSQL schema for a SaaS platform. Include tables for profiles, subscriptions, and project workspaces with Row Level Security enabled.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate Prisma schema and run migrations for multi-tenant SaaS'

steps.title: Implement Stripe Billing Logic
steps.description: Connect your application to Stripe webhooks to automate user access and subscription status, removing manual billing overhead.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a robust Stripe webhook handler for Next.js that updates user subscription status in Supabase based on invoice.payment_succeeded and customer.subscription.deleted.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Integrate Stripe webhook endpoint and test with CLI'

steps.title: Rapid Feature Iteration
steps.description: Use Claude Code to build out specific client feature requests by feeding your existing codebase context into the CLI.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my existing codebase and suggest the most efficient way to add a 'Team Invite' feature using Resend for emails.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Implement team invitation flow with email verification'

steps.title: Deployment and CI/CD Pipeline
steps.description: Finalize your pipeline for instant deployment to Vercel, ensuring every push is tested and production-ready.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a GitHub Actions workflow that runs linting, type-checking, and unit tests on every pull request to main.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Configure Vercel deployment and CI/CD pipeline'

pro_tips: Always use a 'Context File' to summarize your project architecture so Claude Code doesn't get lost in massive node_modules folders.
Hard-code your 'Golden Path'—the exact sequence of features that converts a free user to a paid subscriber—and build the UI around it first.
Ignore complex state management tools like Redux; stick to React Query for server-state to reduce bundle size and complexity.
Use mock data factories for your initial dev cycle so you can test complex UI states without waiting for backend API responses.
Treat your starter kit as an evolving asset; every time you build a unique feature, modularize it into a reusable component library for the next client.
content.next_steps: After completing your first build, document your 'standard stack' and create a private repository template. This allows you to spin up a new client project in under 10 minutes, effectively doubling your hourly rate.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting your skills sit idle while clients wait for solutions. You have the tools, the workflow, and the blueprint to dominate the freelance market. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
